title: sorbus dacica
intro: Sorbus dacica, also known as Dacica whitebeam, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Balkan Peninsula, with medicinal properties attributed to its bark, leaves, and fruits. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Sorbus dacica can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, or used in the form of dried flowers, leaves, and bark in traditional medicine.
